ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF
THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN
IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON
TUESDAY , MARCH THE FIRST , 1949 , AND ENDED
WEDNESDAY , MARCH THE THIRTIETH , 1949 .
CHAPTER 1
(H. B. 1)
AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Expenditure Fund for
the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1949 , for expenses of sessions of the
General Assembly .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. There is hereby appropriated out of the funds in the
General Expenditure Fund not otherwise appropriated for
the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1949 , the sum of $ 110,000 for
expenses of Sessions of the General Assembly.
2. Whereas there is no appropriation made for the ex-
penses of Legislative Sessions for the fiscal year ending June
30 , 1949 , and it is necessary that funds be immediately pro-
vided for this Extraordinary Session of the General Assem-
bly, an emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall take
effect from and after its passage and approval by the Gover-
nor .
Approved March 10 , 1949 .
CHAPTER 2
(H. B. 2 )
AN ACT relating to the ad valorem tax system of the state , counties ,
cities and other taxing districts and making an appropriation for
the purpose .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :

6 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
SECTION 1. Section 132.230 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Every person listing his property with the county
tax commissioner shall state :
(a ) Each separate tract of land , with the number of
acres in each tract ; the value per acre ; each of the improve-
ments thereon ; the name of the nearest resident thereto ;
where located , giving the election precinct in which it is lo-
cated ; the number of each city lot and the improvements
thereon , in what city , on which street , the value of each , and
the value of the improvements thereon to the extent that they
enhance the value of each lots ; whether there is any land
adjoining his owned by a nonresident of the county or state ,
giving the name and place of residence of any such owner ,
if known ;
(b) The number of livestock , their type , species and
value ;
( c ) All other property including the number , denomina-
tion and fair cash value of all bonds subject to taxation
owned by him with the value thereof on January 1 of the
year for which the assessment is made , unless otherwise pro-
vided by law ; and
(d ) Such other facts as may be required in the blanks
provided .
(2) An error or informality in the description or loca-
tion of the property , or in the name of the owner or person
assessed , shall not invalidate the assessment if the property
can with reasonable certainty be located or identified from
the description given , in which case the collector may receive
the taxes and by his receipt correct the error or informality .
SECTION 2. Section 132.290 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Any property which has not been listed for taxa-
tion , for any year in which it is taxable , by the time the
board of supervisors completes its work for that year shall
be deemed omitted property .

CHAPTER 2 7
( 2 ) Omitted property consisting of money in hand ,
notes , bonds , shares of stock , accounts or other credits , se-
cured or unsecured may be assessed retroactively , either
voluntarily or by action , at any time within ten years from
the date when it became omitted . All other omitted property
may be assessed retroactively in the manner provided by
law at any time within five years from the date when it be-
came omitted , but the lien thereby accruing on any such
property except real property shall not prejudice the rights
of bona fide purchasers acquired in the meantime .
(3 ) All omitted property that should have been assessed
prior to July 1 , 1949 , shall be subject to the same penalties
and rate of interest as provided by law for the years when
the property was not assessed . All omitted property that
should have been assessed as of or subsequent to July 1 , 1949 ,
shall be subject to a penalty of ten per cent of the amount
of the taxes , and interest at one per cent per month from
the date when the taxes would have become delinquent had
the property been listed as required by law , until the date
the tax bill is prepared in accordance with KRS 133.230 .
When the property is assessed retroactively by action prose-
cuted in the manner provided by KRS 132.330 and 132.340 ,
an additional penalty of twenty per cent of the amount of
the original tax , interest and penalty shall be collected for the
purpose provided in KRS 134.400 and paid into the State
Treasury . All other penalties and interest shall be dis-
tributed in the same manner as the tax .
SECTION 3. Section 132.310 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any person who has failed to list for taxation any
omitted property consisting of real property or tangible per-
sonal property , except such as is subject to assessment by
the Kentucky Tax Commission , may at any time list such
omitted property with the county tax commissioner . The tax
commissioner shall proceed to assess the property and shall
within ten days from the date the property was listed notify
the taxpayer of the amount of the assessment . The notice
shall be given as provided in KRS 132.450 ( 2 ) . The tax-

8 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
payer may appeal the assessment to the Kentucky Tax
Commission within fifteen days from the date of notice , in
the same manner as an appeal from the final action of the
county board of supervisors and may appeal from the action
of the Kentucky Tax Commission as provided in KRS
133.120 . If no appeal is taken within fifteen days from the
date of notice , the assessment as made by the county tax
commissioner shall become final and shall be certified by the
tax commissioner to the county clerk. Omitted property
listed for taxation under this section shall be subject to the
penalties provided in KRS 132.290 ( 3 ) .
SECTION 4. Section 132.360 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any assessment of accounts receivable , notes , stocks
or bonds that were listed with the county tax commissioner
may be reopened by the Department of Revenue within two
years after the date as of which they were assessed , unless
the assessed value thereof is the face value in the case of
accounts receivable and notes or the quoted value in the case
of stocks and bonds , or has been established by a court of
competent jurisdiction . If upon reopening the assessment
the department finds that the assessment was less than the
fair cash value and should be increased , it shall give notice
thereof to the taxpayer , who may within twenty days there-
after protest to the department and offer evidence to show
that no increase should be made . After the department has
disposed of the protest , the taxpayer may appeal from any
such additional assessment as provided in KRS 131.110
and 131.120 .
(2 ) Upon such assessment becoming final the depart-
ment shall notify the county tax commissioner and shall
certify the amount of increase in the assessment to the county
court clerk for the preparation of a tax bill therefor , which
shall be prepared in the same manner and subject to the same
conditions as in the case of omitted tax bills . The tax bill
shall be handled and collected as an omitted tax bill , and
the additional tax shall be subject to the same penalties as
the tax on omitted property .

CHAPTER 2 9
SECTION 5. Section 132.570 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) No person shall willfully make a false statement ,
or , to avoid taxation , make a temporary investment in securi-
ties exempt from taxation , or convert any intangible property
into nontaxable property outside of this state , or resort to
any device to evade taxation . Any person doing so shall be
subject to three times the amount of tax upon his property ,
to be recovered by the sheriff by action in the name of the
Commonwealth in the county in which the property is liable
for taxation , or by the Department of Revenue , when the
taxes are payable to it , in the Franklin circuit or quarterly
court .
( 2 ) No person shall transfer or assign of record any
mortgage note , bond or other evidence of indebtedness , se-
cured by any recorded instrument , for the sole purpose of
evading the taxes thereon .
SECTION 6. Section 132.990 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any person who willfully fails to supply the county
tax commissioner or the Department of Revenue with a com-
plete list of his property and such facts with regard thereto
as may be required or who violates any of the provisions of
KRS 132.570 shall be fined not more than five hundred
dollars .
(2 ) Any county tax commissioner who willfully fails
or neglects to perform any duty legally imposed upon him
shall be fined not more than five hundred dollars for each
offense , and the judgment of conviction may declare his
office vacant .
( 3) Any county clerk who willfully fails or neglects to
perform any duty required of him by KRS 132.480 or by
KRS 132.490 shall be fined not more than fifty dollars for
each offense .
SECTION 7. Section 133.120 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :

10 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) Any taxpayer aggrieved by an assessment made by
the tax commissioner may appeal to the board of supervisors .
A letter or other written petition stating the reasons for
appeal shall be filed with the county court clerk between the
date the inspection period begins and the date the board of
supervisors convenes .
( 2 ) The board of supervisors shall hold a public hear-
ing for all appeals in protest of the action of the tax commis-
sioner , and after hearing all the evidence , shall make such
changes as it deems necessary to equalize assessments . The
board shall report to the tax commissioner any property
omitted from the tax roll . The tax commissioner shall assess
such property and immediately give notice to the taxpayer
in the manner required by KRS 132.450 ( 2 ) , specifying a
date when the board of supervisors will hear the taxpayer ,
if he so desires , in protest of the action of the tax com-
missioner .
( 3 ) The board of supervisors shall have power to issue
subpoenas , compel the attendance of witnesses , and adopt
rules and regulations concerning the conduct of its business .
Any member of the board shall have power to administer
oaths to any witness in proceedings before the board .
(4) Any taxpayer feeling himself aggrieved by the final
action of the board of supervisors may within fifteen days
after its adjournment appeal to the Kentucky Tax Commis-
sion by filing a statement of the grounds for his appeal and
a certified copy of the action of the board of supervisors .
The tax commissioner , the county judge , the Department of
Revenue , the chief finance officer of any city using the county
assessment , if the city elects to do so , or the superintendent
of any school district in which property reviewed by the
board of supervisors is located , may appeal to the Tax Com-
mission in the same manner as a taxpayer . The party mak-
ing the appeal shall give notice in the manner provided by
the Civil Code of Practice to all other parties entitled by
this section to appeal . The Tax Commission , sitting in the
county in which the property is located shall hear the evi-
dence de novo and fix a value upon the property consistent
with sections 171 , 172 and 174 of the Constitution . The

CHAPTER 2 11
Commissioner of Revenue may appoint a conferee to conduct
any hearings , receive evidence and hear argument , and em-
body same in a report to the Tax Commission . Any of the
parties may appeal from the judgment of the Tax Commis-
sion to the circuit court in the county where the property is
located and thence to the Court of Appeals , as provided by
law . No appeal under this section shall delay the collection
or payment of any taxes based upon the assessment in con-
troversy . The taxpayer shall pay all state , county and
district taxes due on the valuation which he claims as true
value as and when required by law , and when the valuation
is finally determined upon appeal , the court or Tax Commis-
sion shall certify the valuation to the county court clerk and
the taxpayer shall be billed for any additional tax and six
per cent interest from the date when the tax would have
become due if no appeal had been taken . The provisions of
KRS 134.390 shall apply to such tax bill .
( 5) It shall be the duty of the county attorney to repre-
sent the interest of the state and county in all hearings before
the board of supervisors and on all appeals prosecuted from
its decision .
SECTION 8. Section 133.125 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The county clerk shall act as clerk of the board of
supervisors , and he shall keep an accurate record of the pro-
ceedings and orders of the board . The record shall show
the name of the owner of the property , the description , the
type of property , the amount of the assessment the county
tax commissioner placed on the property and the amount of
change made in the assessment by the board of supervisors .
A copy certified by the chairman of the board and attested
by the county clerk shall be filed by the clerk with the tax
commissioner and with the Department of Revenue within
five days after the adjournment of the board .
( 2 ) After receipt of the clerk's certification of the action
of the board of supervisors , the county tax commissioner
shall complete his rolls by adding to them all listings and
assessments which he may have made after the rolls were

12 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
open for inspection as provided in KRS 133.045 and making
all adjustments ordered by the board of supervisors , and he
shall turn the rolls over to the clerk within ten days after
the board adjourns . Whereupon the clerk shall make a re-
capitulation of the assessment by taxing districts and types
of property and shall submit such recapitulation to the De-
partment of Revenue not later than June 1 , unless the time
is extended by the department .
( 3 ) The county clerk shall certify to the county court
the number of days during which the board was in session ,
and the court shall enter this fact of record along with the
amount due the supervisors for their services . On a pre-
sentation of a copy of the order , the Department of Finance
shall draw a warrant on the State Treasurer in favor of the
supervisors and clerk for the amount due for their services .
(4) The clerk shall be allowed the same compensation
per day for his services as is allowed to members of the
board of supervisors of his county , and he shall be paid in
the same manner as members of the board of supervisors
are paid . Provided , that the clerk shall be allowed compensa-
tion for not more than ten days after adjournment of the
board of supervisors for completing and filing the record of
the board and for preparing the recapitulation .
SECTION 9. Section 133.180 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
When the Kentucky Tax Commission has completed its
action on the assessment of the property in any county , the
Department of Revenue shall , not later than June 20 , charge
the amount of taxes due from the county to the sheriff of the
county , and certify to the county clerk the assessment as it
is approved by the Kentucky Tax Commission and the amount
of taxes due . When any item of property is in process of
appeal as provided in KRS 133.120 , and the valuation has
not been finally determined , the certification of such property
shall be based on the valuation claimed by the taxpayer as
the true value . The county clerk shall affix the certification
to the tax books and enter it of record in the order book , and

CHAPTER 2 13
it shall be the sheriff's or collector's warrant for the collec-
tion of taxes .
SECTION 10. Section 133.185 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
No tax rate for any taxing district imposing a levy upon
the county assessment shall be determined before the assess-
ment is certified by the Kentucky Tax Commission to the
county court clerk as provided in KRS 133.180 .
SECTION 11. Section 133.220 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The Department of Revenue annually shall furnish
to each county clerk tax bill forms designed for adequate
accounting control sufficient to cover the taxable property
on the rolls . These tax bill forms shall be furnished not later
than ten days after the Kentucky Tax Commission has ren-
dered its certification . The county annually shall furnish the
county clerk with sufficient tax bill forms for his use in the
preparation of tax bills for all taxpayers who are liable for a
poll tax and who own no property assessed for taxation .
( 2 ) After receiving the forms the county clerk shall
make out for the use of the sheriff or collector a correct tax
bill for each taxpayer in the county whose property has been
assessed and whose valuation is included in the certification
provided in KRS 133.180 . If the bills are bound the cost of
binding shall be paid out of the county levy . Each tax bill
shall show the rate of tax upon each one hundred dollars
worth of property for state , county and school purposes ;
the name of the taxpayer ; the number of acres of farm land
and its value ; the number of lots and their value ; the amount
and value of notes and money ; the value of mixed personal
property ; the county poll ; the school poll ; the school tax ;
and the total amount of taxes due the state , county , school
fund and other levies . The county clerk shall attest each bill
by signing it officially . Provision shall be made for the sher-
iff to have a stub , duplicate or other proper evidence of
receipt of payment of each tax bill .
(3 ) Tax bills prepared in accordance with the certifica-

14 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion of the Kentucky Tax Commission shall be delivered to
the sheriff or collector by the county clerk before September
15 of each year . The clerk shall take a receipt showing the
number of tax bills and the total amount of tax due each tax-
ing district as shown upon the tax bills . The receipt shall
be signed and acknowledged by the sheriff or collector before
the county clerk, filed in the county court , and recorded in the
order book of the county court in the manner required by
law for recording the official bond of the sheriff .
SECTION 12. Section 133.230 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Upon receipt of a certification of omitted property by
the tax commissioner , or by the Department of Revenue , the
county clerk shall make out for the use of the sheriff or col-
lector a tax bill for each taxpayer who owes omitted taxes .
The omitted tax bills shall be attested by the clerk in the same
manner as the tax bills described in KRS 133.220 . The clerk
shall deliver the omitted tax bill to the sheriff or collector as
soon as the omitted poll or property has been finally assessed .
SECTION 13. Section 132.580 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is repealed .
SECTION 14. All laws or parts of laws inconsistent
with the provisions of this Act are hereby repealed to the
extent of such conflict .
SECTION 15. There is hereby appropriated out of the
General Expenditure Fund for the Department of Revenue ,
for the remainder of the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1949
and without lapse to the General Fund until June 30 , 1950 ,
the sum of $ 150,000 , in addition to any other appropriations
made at the Session of the General Assembly at which this
Act is enacted , for the purpose of administering laws relat-
ing to the assessment of property for taxes , subject to the
provisions of Chapters 12 , 42 , and 45 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes .
SECTION 16. The provisions of this Act shall be sev-

CHAPTER 3 15
erable , and if any Section , Subsection , Subdivision , Para-
graph , Sentence or Clause of this Act is held invalid or
unconstitutional such decision shall not affect the remaining
portions of this Act .
SECTION 17. Sections 3, 4, 7, 8, end 12 of this Act
shall not become operative until January 1 , 1950 .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .
CHAPTER 3
(H. B. 3)
AN ACT relating to the ad valorem tax system of the state , counties ,
cities and other taxing districts and to the office of county tax
commissioner and making an appropriation for the purpose .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 132.370 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) There shall be a county tax commissioner in each
county in lieu of a county assessor .
(2 ) County tax commissioners shall be elected at the
same time as provided for assessors in section 99 of the
Constitution , and shall possess the qualifications required by
section 100 of the Constitution and by KRS 132.380 . They
shall take office the first Monday in December and shall
serve for a term of four years and until the election and
qualification of their successors , except that tax commis-
sioners elected in the year 1949 shall take office the first
Monday in January , 1950 , and shall serve until the first Mon-
day in December , 1953 , and until the election and qualification
of their successors . County tax commissioners shall be
eligible for re-election .
( 3 ) A county tax commissioner may be removed from
office by the circuit court of his county , upon petition of the
department or any taxpayer , for willful disobedience of any
just or legal order of the department , or for misfeasance or

16 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
malfeasance in office or willful neglect in the discharge of
his official duties , but he or the petitioner may appeal to the
Court of Appeals .
Section 2. Section 132.380 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Before any person's name shall be placed upon any
ballot as a candidate for the office of county tax commis-
sioner in any primary , or general election held in 1949 or
in any year thereafter , except as a candidate to succeed him-
self in office , or before he may be appointed county tax com-
missioner , he shall hold a certificate issued by the Department
of Revenue in 1949 or thereafter , showing that he has been
examined by it and that he is qualified for the office . All
certificates issued in 1949 or thereafter shall expire one year
from the date of issuance . The examinations shall be both
written and oral , and formulated so as to test fairly the
ability and fitness of the applicant to serve as county tax
commissioner . The Department of Revenue shall hold such
examinations in at least one place in each Congressional
District within thirty days from the effective date of this
Act , in the year 1949 , and every four years thereafter during
the month of February . If there is only one person qualified
to be a candidate in the county , examinations shall be held
again prior to the filing date . The Department of Revenue
shall advise each county attorney of the time and place of
the examination and the county attorney shall post a notice
thereof in a conspicuous place in the courthouse two weeks
before such examination is given . Any person desiring to
take such examination shall appear at the time and place
designated .
(2) Whenever there is a vacancy in the office of county
tax commissioner to be filled by appointment or by election ,
and there is not more than one person holding a valid cer-
tificate and eligible for appointment or election , or whenever
the county judge requests it , the Department of Revenue
may hold a special examination for applicants seeking a
certificate for the office . Such special examination shall be
held in the same manner as regular examinations .

CHAPTER 3 17
(3 ) Examinations shall be given and graded in accord-
ance with rules of the department published to the applicants
at the time of the examination . Within thirty days after the
examination , a certificate of fitness and qualification to fill
the office of county tax commissioner shall be issued by the
Department of Revenue to each person passing the exami-
nation .
(4 ) Examination records shall be preserved by the
department for twelve months after the examination , and
the record of any person who took the examination may be
seen by him at the office of the Department of Revenue in
Frankfort , Kentucky .
Section 3. Section 132.400 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Before entering upon the duties of his office , the county
tax commissioner shall execute a bond conditioned upon the
faithful performance of the duties of his office with a surety
to be approved by the Department of Revenue . In counties
containing a city of the first class , the bond shall be in the
sum of ten thousand dollars ; in counties containing a city of
the second class , five thousand dollars ; in all other counties ,
two thousand dollars .
Section 4. Section 132.410 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The fiscal court of each county shall provide for the
county tax commissioner a suitable office room or rooms in
the county courthouse , or when that is not practicable , in
some other building at the county seat, together with suitable
furniture . In that office shall be safely kept the books , maps ,
taxpayers ' lists , papers and all other records pertaining to
the assessment of property within the county , except when
such records are required by law to be placed in the custody
of other officers . The provisions of KRS 432.130 shall apply
to these public records .
( 2 ) The county tax commissioner shall engage in official
duties at least five days a week during regular working hours

18 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and shall keep scheduled office hours at least five days each
week .
Section 5. Section 132.420 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The county tax commissioner shall , subject to the
direction , instruction and supervision of the Department of
Revenue , make the assessment of all property in his county
except as otherwise provided , prepare property assessment
records , and have such other powers and duties relating to
assessment as may be prescribed by law or by the de-
partment .
(2 ) The department may require the tax commissioner
to be present when safe deposit boxes or similar receptacles
are opened under the provisions of KRS 140.250 .
Section 6. Section 132.440 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The county tax commissioner or his deputies shall read
and administer to every person listing property the following
oath : " You swear that the list of taxable property given by
you contains a full and complete list of all of your property
and of all the property in your possession which is not other-
wise listed as of the assessment date , and that a fair cash
value has been placed on all such property required to be
valued . ”

CHAPTER 4
( H. B. 4 )
AN ACT relating to the ad valorem tax system of the state , counties ,
cities and other taxing districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 132.030 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Every person having on July 1 , 1949 or on Septem-
ber 1 of any year thereafter a deposit in any bank , trust
company or combined bank and trust company organized
under the laws of this state , or in any national bank of this
state , shall pay an annual tax to the state equal to one -tenth
of one per cent upon the amount of the deposit , and no de-
duction therefrom shall be made for any indebtedness . The
tax shall be paid to the Department of Revenue by the bank
or company with which the deposit is made , as the agent of
the depositor , on or before September 1 , 1949 and thereafter
on or before November 1 next following the date of the report
provided for in KRS 132.040 .
( 2 ) No other tax shall be assessed by the state or any
county , city or other taxing district on such deposits or
against the depositor on account of such deposits .
Section 2. Section 132.040 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :

26 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) Each bank , trust company , combined bank and trust
company and national bank doing business in this state shall
file with the department of Revenue on or before July 21 ,

a report setting forth the total amount of its deposits as of
the preceding July 1 , 1949 and thereafter as of the preceding
September 1 that would be taxable in the name of the de-
positor under the laws of this state , and shall , on or before
September 1 , 1949 and thereafter on or before November 1
of each year , pay to the department one -tenth of one per cent
of the amount of such deposits , and may charge to and de-
duct from the deposit of each depositor the amount of the
tax so paid on his behalf. A lien is hereby given to the banks
and trust companies on the funds belonging to the respective
depositors on which the tax has been so paid . Any claim for
taxes against the depositor by the bank or trust company
paying the taxes shall be asserted within six months after
the payment of the taxes to the department , and all claims
or liens therefor shall be thereafter barred .
( 2 ) Each bank or trust company that willfully fails to
make the returns or to pay the taxes on behalf of its de-
positors shall forfeit to the state an amount equal to one-
tenth of one per cent of such deposits for each month's delay
in filing the return or in paying the tax . The tax and for-
feitures may be recovered in an action commenced by the
county attorney in the county court of the county in which
the bank or trust company is located , but if the Department
of Revenue or the court in which the action is heard becomes
satisfied that the failure was not willful , the department or
the court may waive any part or all of the penalty .

CHAPTER 6
(H. B. 6 )
AN ACT relating to the distribution of the common school equalization
fund .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :

46 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 1. Section 157.051 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
As used in KRS 157.051 to 157.055 :
( 1 ) " Equalization fund " means a special fund of 10
per cent of the total appropriation for the common school
fund , appropriated by the General Assembly for the specific
purpose of equalizing educational services in the school dis-
tricts of Kentucky .
( 2 ) " Gross assessment " means the total assessment of
all property in a school district , subject to local school taxes ,
based upon the assessment as certified by the Kentucky Tax
Commission and an estimate made by the Department of
Revenue of the franchise assessment and the assessment of
other property subject to local school taxes in each school
district . If such assessment has not been certified by the
Kentucky Tax Commission by June 7 of any year , then the
Department of Revenue shall on the basis of the county tax
commissioners ' assessment and such other information as
may be available make an estimate of the assessment of all
property subject to local school taxes in each school district
and this estimate shall be used as the gross assessment .
(3 ) " Potential revenue " means the sum obtained for a
given school year by adding together the following :
( a) The revenue that may accrue by applying to the
gross assessment , the maximum tax rate authorized by the
General Assembly for a school district other than for sinking
fund purposes , except that for the school year 1949-50 , any
authorized maximum tax rate in excess of $ 1.25 shall , in the
case of county school districts , be deemed to be $1.25 for the
purposes of this paragraph .
(b ) The amount distributable to a school district on a
census pupil basis as provided in Section 186 of the Con-
stitution .
(c) The amount of any other revenue which can reason-
ably be anticipated to recur in a school district .
(4 ) "Arithmetic mean index " means the quotient ob-
tained when the sum of the average daily membership and

CHAPTER 6 47
the average daily attendance of public school pupils residing
in a school district is divided by two .
(5 ) " Net ability index " means the quotient obtained
when the potential revenue of a school district is divided by
the arithmetic mean index of such school district .
(6 ) " Assessment ratio " means the ratio of the assessed
value to fair cash value of property assessed by the county
tax commissioner in a school district , determined in the man-
ner as provided in KRS 133.150 .
(7) " Tax effort index " means the result obtained when
a school district's assessment ratio is multiplied by such
school district's tax rate and the product thereof is divided
by a mathematical constant . The mathematical constant for
the school year beginning on July 1 , 1949 , shall be .90 and
shall increase yearly by .05 until it becomes 1.20 , at which
figure it shall remain until otherwise provided by law . A tax
effort index of greater than one shall be considered as one .
(8 ) " Maximum equalization allotment " means the
amount each participating district could receive from the
equalization fund if all the participating districts had a tax
effort index of one.
Section 2. Section 157.052 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The equalization fund shall be administered and dis-
tributed under the direction of the Superintendent of Public
Instruction , with the approval of the State Board of Educa-
tion , as provided in KRS 157.053 to 157.055 .
Section 3. Section 157.053 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The Department of Revenue shall furnish annually
to the Superintendent of Public Instruction the gross assess-
ment and the assessment ratio of each school district .
( 2 ) The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall
make a careful study to determine annually the net ability
index for each school district in Kentucky . After all school
districts in the state have been arranged in a rank order

48 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
from high to low according to their net ability indices , the
Superintendent of Public Instruction , with the approval of
the State Board of Education , shall determine the maximum
equalization allotment to each district so that when the total
potential revenue of all participating districts , plus the
equalization fund , is divided by the total arithmetic mean
indices of all participating districts , it will give the same
potential amount of money per pupil per year represented in
the arithmetic mean index in each participating district .
( 3 ) Each school district shall be allotted such part of the
equalization fund as may be determined by multiplying the
district's tax effort index by the district's maximum equaliza-
tion allotment , provided , that a district which maintains an
assessment ratio equal to or greater than that determined
for the assessment made as of July 1 , 1948 , and levies the
maximum tax rate authorized by the General Assembly, shall
receive : not less than 95 per cent of the district's maximum
equalization allotment for the school year beginning July 1 ,
1949 ; not less than 90 per cent for the school year beginning
July 1 , 1950 ; not less than 80 per cent for the school year be-
ginning July 1 , 1951 ; not less than 70 per cent for the school
year beginning July 1 , 1952 ; not less than 50 per cent for the
school year beginning July 1 , 1953 ; and , not less than 25 per
cent for the school year beginning July 1 , 1954 , and there-
after , no school district shall receive any distribution from
the equalization fund unless it has a tax effort index of one
for the year of distribution .
(4 ) So much of the equalization fund which would not
be distributed under subsection (3 ) , due to the failure of
one or more participating districts to meet the full require-
ments of a tax effort index of one shall be applied so as to
raise the state equalization level and shall be distributed in
accordance with the general statistical principles set out in
subsections ( 2) and ( 3 ) .
Section 4. Section 157.054 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The equalization fund shall be allotted to the local
boards of education as provided in KRS 157.052 to 157.055

CHAPTER 6 49
by the Superintendent of Public Instruction with the approval
of the State Board of Education , and shall be distributed to
the local boards of education regularly .
Section 5. Section 157.055 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The Superintendent of Public Instruction with the ap-
proval of the State Board of Education shall prescribe rules
and regulations governing the administration and expendi-
ture of any moneys allotted to local school districts from the
equalization fund . The money allotted to any local board
of education under the terms of KRS 157.052 to 157.055 shall
be received , held , and expended by it under the same liability
and responsibility as provided by law for other funds which
come into the hands of such board.
Section 6. The provisions of this Act shall be severable ,
and if any Section , Sub - section , Sub -division , Paragraph ,
Sentence , or Clause of this Act is held invalid or unconstitu-
tional , such decision shall not affect the remaining portions
of this Act .
Section 7. WHEREAS , it is estimated that 71 school dis-
tricts will be eliminated from school equalization aid under
the 1948 Kentucky Statutes , which require the equalization
fund to be allotted to the school districts on or before April
1 , and ,
WHEREAS , as a result , in these school districts , schools
would be closed ; terms would be shortened ; salaries would
be reduced ; there would be default on numerous school bond
issues ; there would be a mass elimination of school transpor-
tation and school building construction would be stopped , and ,
WHEREAS , it will be necessary , for the administration
of this Act , to prepare immediately for the execution of the
amended law on the distribution of the equalization fund , in
order that all participating districts may receive their fair
share of aid from the equalization fund ,

50 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
NOW , THEREFORE , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist and this Act shall become a law and take effect im-
mediately upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .

tax assessor . If the school levy is to be made upon the county
assessment , the county clerk shall furnish to the proper
school district or districts the assessed valuation of property
subject to local taxation in such district or districts as certi-
fied by the Kentucky Tax Commission .
Section 2. Section 160.470 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Each district board of education shall prepare a
general school budget on forms prescribed and furnished by
the State Board of Education , showing the amount of money
needed for current expenses , debt service , capital outlay, and
other necessary expenses of the schools during the succeed-
ing fiscal year , the estimated total amount that will be re-
ceived from the common school fund and other sources , the
assessed valuation of property subject to local taxation in
the school district furnished as provided in KRS 160.460
subsection ( 3 ) , an estimate made by the Department of
Revenue of franchise assessments and assessments of other
property in the school district not furnished under the pro-
visions of KRS 160.460 subsection ( 3 ) and the amount that
will be needed to be raised by local taxation , including the
rate of levy necessary to raise such an amount . Three copies
of the budget shall be filed with the clerk of the tax levying
authority of the district within three days after the school
district has received its assessment data . The clerk shall
present the budget to the proper tax levying authority when
the authority convenes to fix its school levy . The failure of
the district board of education to furnish the budget within
the time prescribed shall not invalidate any levy made there-
after . Within three days after the tax levying authority has
fixed the tax rate , the district board of education shall for-
ward two copies of the budget to the State Board of Educa-
tion for its approval or disapproval . The budget shall be
disapproved if it is financially unsound or fails to comply

52 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
with the law and the State Board of Education shall state its
reason for disapproval . In such event the district board of
education shall amend its budget to obviate the reasons for
disapproval . If the budget as amended specifies a tax levy
different from that levied under the disapproved budget , the
tax levying authority shall amend its levy in accordance
therewith, so that the levy as amended shall comply with the
provisions of KRS 160.460 subsection ( 1 ) . No general school
budget shall become effective until approved by the State
Board of Education .
( 2 ) Each district board of education shall , if required
by the State Board of Education , prepare and submit to it
not later than September 15 , a close estimate or working bud-
get which shall conform to the rules and regulations pre-
scribed by the State Board of Education , and which shall be
consistent in its major divisions with the general school bud-
get previously prepared .
WHEREAS , Sections 160.460 and 160.470 of the 1948
Kentucky Revised Statutes require an annual school levy to be
made in April and further require copies of the budget to be
filed with the clerk of the tax levying authority on or before
March 20 , and ,
WHEREAS , it will be necessary that such requirements
be immediately superseded due to the impossibility of secur-
ing accurate assessments , equalization data and other neces-
sary information at such times , and ,
WHEREAS , this Act requires the school levy to be made
not later than July 1 and further requires that copies of the
general budget shall be filed with the clerk of the tax levying
authority prior to the fixing of the levy ,
NOW , THEREFORE , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist and this Act shall become a law and take effect imme-
diately after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .

CHAPTER 9
( H. B. 9 )
AN ACT relating to the obligations of rural electric cooperative corpora-
tions , with particular reference to the period of maturity thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 279.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Any corporation formed under this chapter may
issue its obligations and pledge its future revenues for the
payment thereof . The obligations may be in the form of
bonds , notes , debentures , interim certificates or other evi-
dences of indebtedness . The obligations shall be authorized

54 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
by resolution of the board of directors , after a resolution is
first passed by a majority of the common stockholders giving
the board that power . The resolution of the board shall fix
the dates of issuance and maturity , the rate and time of pay-
ment of interest , the denominations , the form ( either coupon
or registered ) , the registration privileges , the manner of
execution , the place and medium of payment , and the terms
of redemption , but the period of maturity shall not exceed
thirty -five years , or the rate of interest six percent , and the
obligations shall not be made redeemable at more than par
and accrued interest .
( 2 ) The obligations may be issued for money or prop-
erty , at public or private sale , for such price as the board
determines , but the interest cost to maturity of the money
or property received for any issue of the obligations shall
not exceed six percent per annum , payable semi -annually , the
value of the property for this purpose to be determined con-
clusively by the board .
(3 ) Pending the preparation or execution of definitive
obligations , interim receipts or certificates or temporary
obligations may be delivered to the purchaser of the obliga-
tions . All obligations and interest coupons issued pursuant
to this chapter shall be negotiable instruments .
(4 ) Any corporation formed under this chapter may
make such covenants or agreements and do such other things
as may be necessary , convenient or desirable in order to
secure its obligations , or that the board believes will tend to
make the obligations more marketable , notwithstanding that
such covenants , agreements or other things may constitute a
limitation on the exercise of the powers granted by this
chapter .
(5 ) Any corporation organized under this chapter may ,
out of any funds available therefor , purchase or redeem any
obligation issued by it at a price not exceeding the principal
amount and accrued interest . All obligations so purchased
shall be canceled and retired .
§ 2. WHEREAS , the present limited period of maturity
of obligations of rural electric cooperative corporations pre-

CHAPTER 10 55
vents such corporations from securing the full advantage of
loans under Federal laws , and therefore such corporations
are threatened with the loss of the right to participate in
Federal loans , an emergency is declared to exist and this Act
shall take effect upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .
CHAPTER 10
(H. B. 10 )
AN ACT relating to engineering and construction services for the State
and its agencies ; abolishing the former Division of Engineering and
Construction of the Department of Welfare , which was transferred
to the Kentucky Building Commission by Chapter 237 of the Acts
of 1948 , and transferring all of its personnel , property , equipment ,
facilities , and appropriations to a new Division of Engineering
created under the State Property and Buildings Commission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. A Division of Engineering , under the supervision ,
control and direction of the State Property and Buildings
Commission , is hereby established . The Division of Engi-
neering and Construction of the Department of Welfare ,
which was created and placed under the direction of the De-
partment of Welfare by Executive - Administrative order of
the Governor of the Commonwealth on October 6 , 1944 , and
which was transferred to the Kentucky Building Commis-
sion by Chapter 237 of the Acts of 1948 , is hereby abolished ,
and all personnel , property , equipment , and facilities of that
division , including its books , records , studies , plans and
specifications shall, upon the effective date of this Act , be
transferred to and be under the supervision and control of
the State Property and Buildings Commission . The State
Property and Buildings Commission shall appoint a qualified
Chief Engineer of its Division of Engineering , who shall
serve as Executive Secretary to the Commission , and who
shall possess such powers and perform such duties as may be
delegated to him from time to time by the Commission . The

56 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Division of Engineering shall, at the direction and request
of the Commssion , prepare such plans and specifications as
may be necessary , investigate and make studies of the com-
parative need and demand for acquiring any lands , or for
the construction or reconstruction or structural maintenance
of any buildings , or for the purchase , installation or construc-
tion of any equipment , facilities or furnishings incidental or
pertaining thereto , shall make such estimates of cost as may
be required in connection with or incidental to the develop-
ment , purchase , acquisition or construction of the foregoing ,
and in general shall give such technical or other assistance
to the Commission and perform such other duties as may be
required , informative or helpful in carrying out the purposes
for which the Commission is created .
§ 2. The appropriation made at the regular 1948 session
of the General Assembly to the Division of Engineering of
the Department of Welfare for the fiscal year 1949-1950 , and
the unexpended and unencumbered balance of the appropria-
tion made to such Division for the fiscal year 1948-1949 , are
hereby transferred to the credit of and shall be considered
as an appropriation to the State Property and Buildings
Commission for purposes of administration and for ordinary
recurring expenses of operation .
§ 3. WHEREAS , doubt exists as to the status and pow-
ers of the existing Division of Engineering , and the urgent
requirements of the State agencies for engineering and con-
struction services demand that engineering and construction
services be made available immediately , an emergency is
declared to exist and this Act shall take effect upon its pas-
sage and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .
CHAPTER 11
(H. B. 11 )
AN ACT relating to property and buildings in Kentucky ; creating a
State Property and Buildings Commission and defining its powers ,
duties , and functions ; making provisions concerning appropriations ;
ratifying action of the former Kentucky Building Commission ;

CHAPTER 11 57
repealing Chapter 237 of the Acts of 1948 and repealing all laws
in conflict .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. As used in this Act , unless the context otherwise
requires :
(1 ) Commission " means the State Property and Build-
ings Commission .
( 2 ) " Real estate " includes lands together with improve-
ments thereon and appurtenances thereto .
(3) " Building " includes any structure or improvement
upon real estate of a permanent nature .
(4) " Building project " includes the construction , re-
construction and structural maintenance of buildings , the
installation of utility services , including roads and sewers ,
and the purchase and installation of equipment , facilities and
furnishings of a permanent nature for buildings .
( 5 ) " State agency " means any State officer , depart-
ment , board , commission , institution , division , or other person
or functional group exercising any function of the State .
Sec . 2. ( 1 ) There is hereby created , as an independent
agency of the State within the meaning of Chapter 12 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes , a State Property and Buildings
Commission composed of the Governor , who shall be chair-
man thereof, the Lieutenant Governor , the Attorney General ,
the Commissioner of Finance , and the Commissioner of
Revenue .
( 2 ) No member of the Commission shall receive any
salary , fee or other remuneration for his services as a mem-
ber of the Commission but each member shall be entitled to
be reimbursed for his ordinary traveling expenses , including
meals and lodging , incurred in the performance of his duties .
Sec . 3. The State Property and Buildings Commission
shall have the power and duty :
( 1 ) To determine the comparative needs and demands
of the various State agencies for acquiring real estate and
for building projects .

58 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 2 ) To expend the capital outlay appropriation , re-
ferred to in Section 5 of this Act , for the purpose of acquiring
real estate and for building projects for any State agency ,
and for matching Federal funds available under public
law No. 725 (79th Congress ) Chapter 958 , Second Session
or any other federal law now existing or hereafter enacted ,
for the purchase , construction , improvement or repairing of
property to be used for public purposes , whether owned by
the Commonwealth , or any agency thereof or by any political
subdivision or municipality , or by non -profit corporations or
associations ; and where so expended for matching federal
funds the provisions of subsection ( 5 ) of this Section and
the provisions of Section 10 of this Act shall not be applicable .
( 3 ) To purchase , lease or rent such real estate as the
Commission may find to be necessary for use by the State
or by any State agency , upon such terms and conditions and
for such consideration as may appear to the Commission to
be fair and reasonable .
(4 ) To sell and convey any real estate owned by the
State or by any State agency , found by the Commission not
to be needed for public use or to have become unsuitable for
such use , except that this power shall not extend to real
estate as to which a particular State agency has the power ,
as recognized in Subsection ( 2 ) of Section 7 of this Act , to
sell or convey with the approval of the Commission .
(5 ) To control the use of any real estate hereafter ac-
quired or improved by the Commission and to determine for
what periods of time and for what purposes any State agency
may use the same , including the agency for whose use it was
initially acquired or improved , and to determine what appro-
priate uses shall be made of such real estate during periods
that the Commission finds the same is not required for the
purposes of any particular State agency .
(5a ) To determine the housing and furnishings needs
of the various State agencies located in Frankfort and to
establish and put into effect a permanent program for hous-
ing them . The commission is also authorized and directed
to allocate office space and furnishings in existing public

CHAPTER 11 59
buildings located in Frankfort according to the needs of the
various agencies and when necessary to provide additional
office space and furnishings in Frankfort under any building
program it deems most advisable and economical for the
state .
(5b ) The permanent housing program shall include
provisions for housing the General Assembly and its related
agencies , including the Legislative Research Commission and
the Statute Revision Commission , the Executive Offices , the

( 6 ) To acquire , by condemnation in any manner pro-
vided in KRS Chapter 416 , any real estate necessary for use
by the State or by any State agency , when the Commission
is unable to agree with the owner thereof on a price for such
real estate .
(7) To lease any real estate or any part thereof, now
owned or hereafter acquired by the State or by any State
agency , to any person not a State agency , when the Com-
mission finds as a matter of fact that the real estate or any
part thereof is not needed for State purposes , except that
this power shall not extend to any real estate as to which a
particular State agency has power , as recognized in sub-
section ( 2 ) of Section 7 of this Act , to lease with the approval
of the Commission . Any such lease shall be upon such terms
and conditions for such consideration as the Commission may
prescribe , and shall be made in the name of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and shall be signed by the chairman of
the Commission and lodged for record in the office of the
Secretary of State .
Sec . 4. After the State Property and Buildings Commis-
sion has determined the need and demand of any State agency
for acquiring real estate or for a building project , the Com-
mission is authorized and empowered to provide for and
adopt such plans and specifications as may be necessary , to

60 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
advertise for and receive bids for any expenditures proposed
to be made , to award contracts for the purpose authorized ,
to supervise construction and make such changes and revisions
in plans and specifications or in construction as may become
necessary , and generally to do any and all other things as
may become necessary or expedient in order to effectively ful-
fill and carry out the purposes for which the Commission is

ployed in order to fulfill the object of its creation , and further

tisement for bids and the receipt thereof and the awarding

ters 12 , 42 and 45. Warrants for such expenditures shall be

Sec . 5. The appropriation made out of the surplus cash

Sec . 6. No state agency shall have power or authority to

or incur any financing commitments , either in the way of a

CHAPTER 11 61
charge against public funds or in the way of negotiations for

stance where it finds that the project is not needed , or that

posed project , may in its discretion elect that the Commission

Sec . 7. No state agency , other than the State Property
and Buildings Commission , shall have power or authority :
(1 ) To acquire , by purchase , lease , condemnation or
other means involving the expenditure of money , any real
estate for the use of the State or of any State agency .
( 2 ) To lease , sell or convey any real estate belonging to
the State or to any State agency , except thatagency , except that any State agency
having the power under the laws to lease , sell or convey real
estate devoted to the exclusive use of such agency may con-
tinue to exercise that power subject to the approval of the
Commission as to each transaction .

Sec . 8. ( 1 ) In carrying out the purpose of this Act , and in
providing for the expenditure of funds for the acquisition of
real estate or for a building project for the State or for any
State agency , the Commission may specify that the cost may
be paid in whole or in part , as may be determined by agree-
ment between the Commission and any State agency (a ) out

nated as capital outlay, or (b ) out of funds directly appro-
priated to the involved State agency which may legally be

mission , by the issuance and sale of revenue bonds by the

or (d ) by any one or a combination of said methods of financ-
ing . When any funds appropriated to or standing to the
credit of a particular State agency are to be used for a pur-
pose of the character above contemplated , the head of the

( 2 ) In any case where the expenditures for the ac-
quisition of real estate or for a building project for the State

(3) The Commission , in providing for the expenditure

fected , or by provision in the trust indenture securing the

CHAPTER 11 63
revenue bonds , such terms and conditions as may be con-
sidered appropriate and reasonable so as to permit the leas-
ing to , and use by, any State agency which provides funds

structed . The Commission , in making any such lease agree-
ment , or in making provision in any trust indenture securing

tions and for such consideration as may appear to be in the

Sec . 9. ( 1 ) The Commission is authorized and empowered
to issue and sell revenue bonds in such denominations and
in such amount or amounts as may be deemed by the Commis-
sion to be for the best interest of the Commonwealth , for any

(a) To acquire real estate to be devoted to a revenue-
producing use .
(b) To pay all or any part of the expense or cost of or

( c ) To defray the cost of plans , specifications , blue
prints , architectural fees and other expenses authorized to be

a pledge and a first lien on all of the receipts and revenue
derived , or to be derived , from the rental or operation of the

the interest thereon issued under the authority of this Act ,
shall constitute an indebtedness of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , nor shall any bond or interest thereon be payable
out of any fund except such funds as may be derived from
rentals or other revenues derived from the operation of the

properties or from such revenues as may have been, or may
be , available for such purpose , or purposes , by law .

under the provisions of this Act shall be sold to the highest

ditional advertising is necessary .

bonds shall be opened and read publicly by the Commission

ury except for the purposes authorized by this Act, together

bonds and other expenses directly related thereto . The Com-
mission may likewise provide in any trust indenture securing
revenue bonds for additional terms and conditions thereof,
or for other restrctions not in conflict with this Act .

CHAPTER 11 65
( 8 ) The authority herein conferred upon the Commis-
sion to issue and sell revenue bonds is intended to , and shall

principles . In the event the Commission determines to aban-
don , or cause to be abandoned , the construction of any build-
ing, or to change the purposes for which such building is

Sec . 11. Any act done or action taken , prior to the effec-
tive date of this Act, by the Kentucky Building Commission
created by Chapter 237 of the Acts of 1948 , which act or ac-
tion was of a character authorized by this Act to be done by
the State Property and Buildings Commission created by this
Act , is hereby ratified and validated the same as if it had

erty and Buildings Commission created by this Act is

(H. R. 1 )
A RESOLUTION appropriating additional compensation for officers and
employees of the House and Senate.
WHEREAS , the General Assembly deems the compensation of the offi-
cers and employees of the Senate and the House of Representatives ,
as provided for in Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1936 , inadequate for the proper functioning of the business of
the Senate and House of Representatives during the 1949 Extraord-
inary Session,
Now , Therefore , be it resolved by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That there is hereby appropriated from the Legislative
Session Fund as additional compensation for the following
officers and employees of the Senate and House of Repre-
sentatives : Assistant Clerk , Enrolling Clerk , Sergeant -at-
Arms , Doorkeeper , Cloakroom Keeper , Janitor and Pages ,
to that compensation as provided for in KRS 6.230 the sum
of One Hundred Seventy -five ( $ 175.00 ) Dollars per diem ,
effective beginning with the first day of the 1949 Extraordi-

nary Session , for each body , for the duration of the 1949
Extraordinary Session .
That the aforesaid amount shall be pro - rated among the
aforementioned constitutional officers and employees , ac-
cording to the per diem set forth in KRS 6.230 , and shall be
by the Department of Finance charged to the account of the
aforenamed officers and employees of the Senate and the
House of Representatives on this basis .
That the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk of the House
of Representatives are directed to issue vouchers to the Com-
missioner of Finance , who shall draw his warrant in favor
of the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk of the House of
Representatives for the above mentioned amounts , and that
the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk of the House of Repre-
sentatives are further directed to distribute the additional
compensation to the above mentioned officers and employees
as provided in KRS 6.230 .
WHEREAS , it is necessary that this additional compen-
sation be received by the officers and employees of the Ex-

expedite the business of the Senate and the House of Repre-
sentatives , an emergency is hereby declared to exist and this
Resolution shall be effective from and after its passage and
approval by the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Approved March 10 , 1949 .

CHAPTER 16
( H. R. 5 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for the payment of the per diem of
the late Isaac M. Blevins .
WHEREAS , the late Isaac M. Blevins was elected to the House of Repre-
sentatives of this General Assembly at the general election in
November 1947 , from the 83rd legislative district ; and
WHEREAS , the deceased spent considerable time and money in his
campaign for nomination and election ; and
WHEREAS , it has heretofore been the custom and precedent of the
General Assembly to allow the widow or dependents of deceased
members the per diem they would have received , and
WHEREAS , if the late Representative Isaac M. Blevins had been per-
mitted to serve in the 1949 Extraordinary Session of the General
Assembly , to which he was duly and legally elected , he would have
received as per diem the sum of $15.00 for each legislative day of
the session as a member thereof ; now therefore
Be it resolved by the House of Representatives of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , the
Senate concurring therein :
That there is hereby appropriated out of the Legislative
Session Fund , available for the present Extraordinary Ses-

CHAPTER 17 71
sion of the General Assembly, an amount equal to $ 15.00 for
each day of the present Extraordinary Session , to be paid to
Mrs. Isaac M. Blevins , widow of the late Representative
Isaac M. Blevins of Wayne County , being the per diem the
late Representative Blevins would have received had he lived
to serve during this session to which he was duly elected .
The Clerk of the House of Representatives shall certify the
amount specified herein to the Commissioner of Finance , who
is hereby directed to draw his warrant upon the State Treas-
urer in the amount specified herein .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .
CHAPTER 17
(H. R. 7)
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for payment of the ministers of the
churches of Frankfort , Kentucky , for performing the duties of
Chaplain for the General Assembly during the present Extraordi-
nary Session .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the sum of $600.00 is hereby appropriated out
of the Legislative Session Fund to the Ministerial Associa-
tion of Frankfort , Kentucky , to be paid in equal shares to
the ministers who performed the duty of Chaplain for the
General Assembly during the present Extraordinary Session .
§ 2. That the Clerk of the House of Representatives shall
certify this Resolution to the Commissioner of Finance , who
shall draw his warrant upon the State Treasurer for the
amount above designated , and the State Treasurer shall issue
his check for the same.
WHEREAS , the ministers of Frankfort , Kentucky , have
been faithful in performing services as Chaplain , and are
entitled to prompt payment , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Resolution shall take effect upon its passage ·
and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .

CHAPTER 18
(H. R. 10 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION for the benefit of officers and employees of the
Senate and House of Representatives ; and declaring an emergency .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That there is hereby appropriated from the
Legislative Session Fund for this Extraordinary Session of
the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
begun on March 1 , 1949 , additional allowances to cover neces-
sary expenses for the following employees and officers of the
Senate and House of Representatives , viz .: Assistant Clerks ,
Enrolling Clerks , Sergeants -at -Arms , Doorkeepers , Cloak-
room Keepers , Janitors and Pages, to that compensation
provided in KRS 6.230 , the sum of $ 4,650.00 .
Section 2. That the Chief Clerk of the Senate shall
requisition and the Commissioner of Finance shall draw a
warrant for the sum of $ 2,232.00 on the State Treasurer of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky in favor of the Chief Clerk
of the Senate . Such Chief Clerk shall forthwith distribute
such expense allowances on the basis of the number of days
that such employees and officers served during this Extraord-
inary Session of the General Assembly in the year 1949 , the
rate per day to be computed by said Chief Clerk and the
Committee on Committees of the Senate on a pro rata basis
of the amounts authorized to be paid them by KRS 6.230 .
Section 3. The Chief Clerk of the House of Representa-
tives shall requisition and the Commissioner of Finance shall
draw a warrant for the sum of $ 2,418.00 on the State Treas-
urer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in favor of the Chief
Clerk of the House of Representatives . Such Chief Clerk
shall forthwith distribute to the employees and officers of the
House of Representatives such expense allowances on the
basis of the number of days that such employees and officers
served during the said Extraordinary Session of the General
Assembly in the year 1949 , the rate per day to be computed

CHAPTER 18 73
by the said Chief Clerk and the Speaker of the House of
Representatives on a pro rata basis of the amounts authorized
to be paid them by KRS 6.230 .
All Acts or parts of Acts in conflict herewith to the ex-
tent of such conflict are hereby repealed .
WHEREAS , the General Assembly will within a short
time adjourn at this Extraordinary Session , and the employ-
ees and officers will depart for various sections of the state ,
an emergency is hereby declared to exist , and this resolution
shall be effective from and after its passage and approval
by the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Approved March 30 , 1949 .
